Home / Glossary
11 — Reference

Field Glossary

Every term used on the rig, in one place. From BHA to WOB — learn these and you'll sound like you've been there.

44 terms and counting
Annular Preventer
Top element of a BOP stack; a rubber sealing element that closes around pipe of nearly any size or on open hole.
BHA
Bottom Hole Assembly. Everything below the drill collars — bit, stabilizers, MWD tools, motors.
Blind Rams
BOP rams that seal a fully open hole (no pipe present) using flat steel faces.
BOP
Blowout Preventer. High-pressure valve system on the wellhead that seals the well in an emergency.
BOSIET
Basic Offshore Safety Induction & Emergency Training. Mandatory for offshore work, includes HUET.
Choke Line
Piping that allows controlled circulation of fluid out of the well while the BOP is closed.
Company Man
The operating oil company's on-site representative with final authority on well decisions.
Derrickman
Works the monkey board high in the derrick, guiding pipe stands and managing mud systems.
Directional Driller
Specialist who steers the bit along a planned trajectory using MWD tools and downhole motors.
Dogleg Severity (DLS)
Measure of how sharply a wellbore curves, in degrees per 100 ft. Excessive DLS increases torque/drag.
Drawworks
The winch system on the rig that raises and lowers the drill string via the traveling block.
Drill Collar
Heavy thick-walled pipe in the BHA that provides weight on bit (WOB).
ECD
Equivalent Circulating Density. Effective mud weight while pumping, including annular friction losses — always higher than static mud weight.
Fracture Pressure
The pressure at which a formation cracks and drilling fluid is lost into the rock. Upper limit of the mud weight window.
H2S
Hydrogen sulfide — a toxic, flammable gas found in sour formations. Extremely dangerous even in small concentrations.
HUET
Helicopter Underwater Escape Training. Core component of BOSIET for offshore workers.
HWDP
Heavy Weight Drill Pipe. Thicker-walled transition pipe between drill pipe and drill collars.
ICP
Initial Circulating Pressure. The standpipe pressure held at the start of a kill circulation.
IWCF
International Well Control Forum — issues globally recognized well control certifications, Levels 2-4.
Kelly
The square or hexagonal pipe historically used to transmit rotation from the rotary table to the drill string; largely replaced by top drives on modern rigs.
Kick
An influx of formation fluid into the wellbore, caused by formation pressure exceeding hydrostatic pressure.
Kill Mud Weight (KMW)
The mud weight required to exactly balance formation pressure after a kick — the target weight for well control operations.
Kill Sheet
The calculation worksheet used during well control to document kick data and the pump schedule for killing the well.
LOT
Leak-Off Test. Pumps fluid into the well after each casing shoe to determine fracture gradient.
LWD
Logging While Drilling. Real-time formation evaluation data (resistivity, density, gamma ray) transmitted during drilling.
Monkey Board
The derrick platform, typically 90-100 ft up, where the derrickman works during tripping operations.
Mud Logger
Analyzes drill cuttings and gas shows in real time to identify formation changes and hydrocarbon indications.
MWD
Measurement While Drilling. Downhole tools transmitting directional data to surface via mud pulse telemetry.
OIM
Offshore Installation Manager. Overall command authority for an offshore installation and its personnel.
Overbalance
The margin by which hydrostatic pressure exceeds pore pressure. Typical safe range: 200-500 psi.
PDC Bit
Polycrystalline Diamond Compact bit — synthetic diamond cutters in a shearing action, the dominant bit type today.
Pipe Rams
BOP rams that close around a specific pipe diameter, sealing the annulus while pipe remains in the hole.
POOH / RIH
Pull Out Of Hole / Run In Hole — tripping the drill string to surface or back into the wellbore.
Pore Pressure
The pressure of fluids within reservoir rock pores. Normal gradient roughly 0.433-0.465 psi/ft.
Roughneck
Rig floor crew member who makes up and breaks out pipe connections. Also called a floorhand.
Roustabout
Entry-level general labor position — cleaning, painting, moving equipment. No prior experience required.
ROP
Rate of Penetration. How fast the bit is drilling, in ft/hr. A sudden increase is called a drilling break.
Shear Rams
Emergency BOP rams that cut through drill pipe and seal the well simultaneously.
SICP
Shut-In Casing Pressure. Recorded on the casing side after shutting in on a kick.
SIDPP
Shut-In Drill Pipe Pressure. Directly reflects excess formation pressure above hydrostatic after shut-in.
Toolpusher
Overall supervisor of the drilling contractor's rig operations, managing all crews, tools, and equipment.
Top Drive
Motor system that rotates the drill string, allowing continuous rotation while adding stands — replaced the kelly on most modern rigs.
Trip Gas
Gas entering the wellbore when pulling the string out of hole due to the swab effect. Monitored by the mud logger.
WOB
Weight on Bit. The portion of drill collar weight applied to the bit to create cutting force.
Looking for something deeper? Many of these terms link to full explainer pages elsewhere on the site — see Well Control, Blowout Preventers, and The Drill String for complete technical breakdowns.

© 2026 RigFloorHQ. Educational and reference content only. Always follow your company's approved procedures, regulatory requirements, and the instructions of your certified well control supervisor. Well control decisions must never be made solely from reference materials.
